summary: Matched about half of `src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c` by replacing a placeholder allocator struct with a more detailed `Allocator`/`Handle` model, implementing multiple lbMemory handle/list/allocation routines, and adding a `STATIC_ASSERT` helper in `platform.h` for struct-size checking. The PR notes that remaining work was mostly register allocation for the submitted functions, with two more functions left for a future PR. Review focused on whether the new static assert macro was clang-compatible and whether it was necessary given checksum-based matching.
what_changed: Expanded `lbmemory.c` from a small placeholder using `lbMemory_UnkStruct1` into a partial decompilation of the lb memory allocator around a static global `struct Allocator g_alloc` corresponding to `lbMemory_804318B0`.

Introduced a `Handle` type with linked-list fields `x0_next`, `x4_lo`, `x8_hi`, and `xC_prev`, plus an `Allocator` type with arena bounds, free-list pointers, allocation counters, and unknown padded regions.

Added a static forward declaration for `lbMemory_80015320` and implemented or substantially filled in functions including `lbMemory_80014E24`, `lbMemory_80014EEC`, `lbMemory_80014F7C`, `lbMemory_80014FC8`, `lbMemory_800150F0`, `lbMemory_8001529C`, `lbMemory_800154BC`, `lbMemory_800154D4`, and `lbMemory_800155A4`.
Added helper macros `PUSH_HANDLE` and `POP_HANDLE` to model allocator freelist/list manipulation in a compact way while preserving codegen-sensitive pointer operations.
Added an inline `new_handle` helper that pops from `g_alloc.x698_free_heap`, checks arena bounds, initializes a `Handle`, and is reused by both `lbMemory_80014E24` and `lbMemory_800154D4`.
Added `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(g_alloc) == 0x6F0)` to guard the reconstructed allocator layout during local build-diff iteration.
Added `STATIC_ASSERT` support to `src/Runtime/platform.h`, including C++ handling around `_Static_assert`/`static_assert`, after review discussion about clang compatibility.
smart_moves: Modeled the allocator as a concrete struct with padding ranges instead of leaving it as raw uintptr fields, making later decompilation more searchable and easier to refine.
Reused `new_handle` for functions that were behaviorally the same except for storing the resulting handle in `g_alloc.x69C`, reducing duplicated allocator initialization logic.
Used named list macros for repeated push/pop linked-list patterns, which documents intent and may help keep delicate codegen stable across multiple functions.
Kept comments on uncertain fields and control-flow issues, such as whether `x8_hi` is a high pointer or size and why iteration starts at `handle->xC_prev`, rather than overclaiming certainty.
Used a size assertion on the reconstructed global allocator to catch padding mistakes early in the build-diff loop.
Deferred remaining functions to a later PR after reviewer suggestion, keeping the merged slice focused on the functions already partially matched.
decomp_lessons: For allocator-like systems, reconstructing the global state object and handle/node structs can unlock many functions at once, even when some field semantics are still unknown.
Padding checks are useful during local struct reconstruction, but project-wide helper macros must remain compatible with clang because clang is used for static analysis and editor language servers.
Checksum matching already catches many struct-size/layout mistakes, so permanent compile-time assertions should justify their maintenance and tooling cost.

Do not assume `__FILE__` literal behavior is trivial: this PR explicitly left `lbMemory_800150F0` as '100% except for literal relocations' and used an external `filename` with comments about relocation differences.
When a decompilation has only register allocation issues left, document that status, but split unrelated remaining functions into future PRs if reviewers recommend it.
Preserve evidence of uncertain semantics with comments and provisional names rather than prematurely renaming fields to misleading domain names.
naming_conventions: Global static allocator was named `g_alloc`, with a comment tying it to symbol `lbMemory_804318B0`.
The reconstructed node type was named `Handle`; review later emphasized that `handle` is also the name of a local variable asserted as truthy in the original/decompiled code context.
Unknown struct fields retained offset-based names such as `x62C_free_mem`, `x630_num_allocs`, `x634_max_num_allocs`, `x698_free_heap`, `x69C`, `x6E0`, `x6E4`, and `x6E8`.
Partially understood pointer bounds used `arenaLo`/`arenaHi` and `x4_lo`/`x8_hi`; the author explicitly questioned whether `x8_hi` was actually a high pointer or size.
Macros used all-caps action names `PUSH_HANDLE` and `POP_HANDLE`, while the helper function used lowercase `new_handle`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit `goto` labels in `lbMemory_800150F0` to preserve a control-flow shape with a null check at the bottom of the loop.
Used pointer casts such as `(Handle*) &h->xC_prev` and `(Handle*) &arg0->xC_prev` to reproduce list traversal/codegen where the previous-pointer field acts like a list sentinel.
Aligned allocation size with `((size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0)`, documenting 32-byte alignment.
Tracked best-fit allocation state with `least_leftover`, `available_space`, `leftover`, and `memp_candidate`, matching the apparent allocator search behavior.
Kept assertion strings and line numbers such as `"_p(free_heap)"`, `"_p(free_mem)"`, `"memp_kouho"`, and `HSD_ASSERT(149, handle)` because these affect matching and evidence about local names.
Used a compile-time size assertion for `sizeof(g_alloc) == 0x6F0` as a build-diff guard against padding/layout drift.
Noted literal relocation mismatch around `OSReport(filename)`/`__assert(filename, 283, "handle")`, signaling that string/file macro lowering can be a matching blocker separate from ordinary logic.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: `clang` did not like the initial `STATIC_ASSERT` macro and reported `declaration does not declare anything`; reviewer asked how the macro worked.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer questioned whether `STATIC_ASSERT` was needed at all because the match checksum already enforces layout-sensitive mistakes.

review_feedback.author: rjeli
review_feedback.feedback: Author explained the old-style negative-array static assertion trick and noted it was useful locally for immediately catching padding miscalculations.

review_feedback.author: rjeli
review_feedback.feedback: Author suggested removing the helper if unnecessary or using a clang-specific `_Static_assert` alternative if kept.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer clarified that clang is used for static analysis, the Issues CI check, and editor language servers.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer found an alternate approach that seemed to work after researching clang-compatible static assert patterns.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Recommended opening a new PR for the remaining two functions rather than expanding this one.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked future changes to refer to commit `5d3bb4bb3cb34960d0aab32de05bfd77f1fcd903`, noting that `handle` is the name of a local variable being asserted as truthy.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete the remaining `lbmemory` functions `lbMemory_8001564C`, `lbMemory_80015320`, or `fn_80015184`.
Check commit `5d3bb4bb3cb34960d0aab32de05bfd77f1fcd903` for the final naming/layout cleanup referenced by reviewer.
Search for subsequent changes to `STATIC_ASSERT` in `src/Runtime/platform.h` to see the final clang-compatible macro form.
Look for later renames or semantic clarification of `Handle.x8_hi`, `x62C_free_mem`, `x698_free_heap`, and `x69C`.
Search for fixes to `lbMemory_800150F0` literal relocation issues around `filename`, `OSReport`, and `__assert`.
